calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Dismissal

Renewed Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Felicia M. Williams's unopposed Renewed Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions is DENIED.

Plaintiff neither timely filed nor timely served their moving papers. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address or place of mailing is outside State of California but within the United States adds 10 calendar days to "any period of notice"]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Plaintiff filed and served their motion on December 10, 2025, which is nowhere near 16 court days plus 10 court days before 12/29/2025, the hearing date noticed.

Plaintiff and Defendant, both, are ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Both sides submit proposed orders.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To (1) Compel Respones To Requests For Admissions, Interrogatories, And Request For Porduction Of Documents; (2) Deem Reqeusts For Admission Admitted; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Felicia Williams 415-214-2400 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. The Court did not receive a notice to contest the court's TR. Ms. williams states she agrees with the TR and has brought her renewed motion. The Court looks at the amended declaration. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FELICIA WILLIAMS' Motion To (1) Compel Respones To Requests For Admissions, Interrogatories, And Request For Porduction Of Documents; (2) Deem Reqeusts For Admission Admitted; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant's unopposed Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery Requests is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE to renew. If Defendant chooses to renew the motion, copies of the relevant discovery requests should be attached as exhibits to her declaration in support of the motion.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Set For Trial On Mar-30-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

*First Amended* Motion To Dismiss Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
June E. Poyourow, Esq. specially for Plaintiff Westlake
Felicia Williams, Esq. 415-214-2400 Defendant in Pro Per.

ARGUED. The Court informs counsel it does not allow special a or courtesy appearances but will make an exception in this case. The Court states Ms. Williams is held to the same standard as counsel. Ms. Williams contests and presents arguments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FELICIA WILLIAMS' Motion To Dismiss Hearing.

Defendant Felicia Williams' motion to dismiss is DENIED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jul-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Felicia Williams 415-214-2400 fwill0333311@gmail.com Defendant in Pro Per (In-person).
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. The Court informs Ms. Williams of the tentative ruling, to refile her motion and to pay attention to the rules. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FELICIA WILLIAMS' Motion To Dismiss.

Off calendar as untimely served. Defendant served the moving papers to an Illinois address on July 2, 2025, only 16 court days before hearing. Defendant failed to extend this notice period to account for service by mail outside the State of California, but within the United States. See CCP 1013.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/DH)
